title: Gymnadenia cucullata
intro: Gymnadenia cucullata, also known as the helmet orchid, is a perennial plant species belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnadenia cucullata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Gymnadenia cucullata is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in combination with other plants to create herbal teas and infusions.
taxonomy: Gymnadenia cucullata is a species of orchid in the subtribe Orchidinae. It belongs to the genus Gymnadenia and the family Orchidaceae. Gymnadenia cucullata is classified as a terrestrial orchid, native to Europe. The species is further divided into several subspecies based on its geographical distribution and morphological characteristics. Gymnadenia cucullata is a relatively rare species, mainly found in mountainous regions and shaded areas.
